rule: hit iff kw>=2 OR sem>=0.7 OR func>=0.7; total_hits>=1 -> FAIL
content_words: audit-first inference logging on edit involvement

results.note: AuditableLLM hash-chain tamper-evident audit trail for adaptation/update/unlearning is a direct functional twin of KINTSUGI-PATCH's append-only visible patch registry with signed-hash provenance; semantic and functional both exceed 0.7.
EditScope traces and reverses edits from model weights, directly covering KINTSUGI-PATCH's edit-trace/reversibility/in-place weight edit pillars; both semantic and functional exceed 0.7.
EditMark uses model editing to embed provenance watermarks in weights, hitting the 'visible seam marker for edit provenance' effect cluster of KINTSUGI-PATCH; sem and func both at threshold.
LarQL SHOW-patches command provides explicit auditable record of every fact changed — direct semantic and functional twin of the patch registry / API-exposed history feature.
Knowledge Editing survey covers ROME/MEMIT and the design space KINTSUGI-PATCH builds on; semantic similarity narrowly exceeds 0.7 (in-place weight edit, edit-trace bookkeeping).
Benchmark of knowledge editing aligns with the in-place weight edit aspect at the 0.7 semantic threshold; counted as hit by sem rule even though functional coverage of registry/audit angle is lower.
Dynamic massive weight editing — overlap with in-place weight edit but no registry, audit, provenance trace, or rollback; below both thresholds.
EasyEdit 2.0 framework with real-time inference-time steering partially covers inference-time exposure of edits and in-place weight edit, putting both sem and func at/above 0.7.

fail_reason: 7 of 8 results clear the sem>=0.7 threshold (AuditableLLM hash-chain audit trail, EditScope edit-tracing+reversal, EditMark watermarking-via-editing, LarQL SHOW-patches auditable record, Knowledge Editing survey, KE benchmark, EasyEdit framework) and 6 additionally clear func>=0.7. KINTSUGI-PATCH's core mechanisms — append-only visible patch registry, inference-time provenance flagging, signed-hash edit-trace, registry rollback over in-place weight edits — are already covered by AuditableLLM (registry+hash), LarQL (SHOW patches), EditScope (trace+reverse), and EditMark (visible weight-edit provenance marker). Total hits = 7 >= 1, so the candidate is not novel.
